days.title: Big and small: meeting adjectives

grammar.title: Adjectives with masculine nouns
grammar.explanation: A Bulgarian adjective changes its ending to **agree** with the noun. The plain (dictionary) form of an adjective is the **masculine** form, used with masculine nouns: **голям град** (big city), **малък стол** (small chair). Notice the verb **to be**: Bulgarian really has one, so you say **Това е голям град** (This is a big city) and **Градът е голям** (The city is big). The forms of *to be* are: **съм, си, е, сме, сте, са**. The adjective usually comes before the noun, just as in English.

reading.title: Дом и стол
reading.text: Това е дом. Домът е голям. А това е стол. Столът е малък.
reading.translation: This is a house. The house is big. And this is a chair. The chair is small.

days.speakingPrompt: Point at two things near you, both with masculine nouns, and describe each one using голям or малък, for example: "Това е голям стол."

days.title: Nice, new, old: more adjectives

vocab.exampleEn: The city is old and beautiful.

grammar.title: More masculine adjectives
grammar.explanation: These new adjectives also take their plain (masculine) form with masculine nouns: **нов дом** (new house), **стар град** (old city), **хубав парк** (nice park). You can chain two adjectives with **и** (and): **стар и хубав град** (an old and beautiful city). To say *very*, put **много** before the adjective: **много хубав** (very nice). Remember the verb *to be*: **Паркът е много хубав** (The park is very nice).

reading.title: Паркът
reading.text: Това е парк. Паркът е голям и хубав. Той е стар, но много хубав.
reading.translation: This is a park. The park is big and beautiful. It is old, but very nice.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a place you like (a park or a city) using two masculine adjectives joined with и, for example: "Градът е стар и хубав."

days.title: Feminine forms: голяма, нова, хубава

grammar.title: Feminine adjectives end in -а
grammar.explanation: Bulgarian adjectives must match the noun's **gender**. With a **feminine** noun (these usually end in **-а** or **-я**, like **къща**, **кола**, **улица**), the adjective adds **-а**: **нов → нова**, **голям → голяма**, **хубав → хубава**, **стар → стара**. So one word, **нов**, becomes **нова кола** (a new car). Note that **голям** becomes **голяма** in the feminine; say it as one smooth word.

reading.title: Улица и кола
reading.text: Това е улица. Улицата е хубава и голяма. А това е кола. Колата е нова.
reading.translation: This is a street. The street is beautiful and big. And this is a car. The car is new.

days.speakingPrompt: Take three feminine nouns (къща, кола, улица) and describe each with a different adjective in its feminine form, for example: "голяма къща, нова кола, хубава улица."

days.title: Neuter and plural: ново, нови

grammar.title: Neuter -о and plural -и
grammar.explanation: With a **neuter** noun (often ending in **-о** or **-е**, like **кафене**, **дете**) the adjective adds **-о**: **ново кафене**, **голямо дете**, **малко дете**. In the **plural** there is just one ending for all genders, **-и**: **нови коли** (new cars), **хубави улици** (beautiful streets), **малки деца** (small children). With plural subjects use **са**: **Колите са нови** (The cars are new). Now you know all four forms: **нов / нова / ново / нови**.

reading.title: Градът
reading.text: Това е град. Тук има нови къщи и хубави улици. Кафенетата са малки, но хубави.
reading.translation: This is a city. Here there are new houses and beautiful streets. The cafés are small, but nice.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a few things you see using a plural adjective with a plural noun, for example: "Тук има нови коли и хубави къщи."

days.title: The definite article on adjectives (fem/neuter/plural)

vocab.exampleEn: Here there are many new houses.

grammar.title: "The" attaches to the adjective
grammar.explanation: When a noun phrase has an adjective, the **definite article** (the *-the* ending) attaches to the **adjective**, not the noun: **нова кола → новата кола** (the new car). The endings match the gender/number: feminine **-та** (**новата**), neuter **-то** (**новото**), plural **-те** (**новите**). The noun then stays in its plain form: **новата кола**, **новото кафене**, **новите къщи**. Compare: without an article it is **нова кола** (a new car); with it, **новата кола** (the new car).

reading.title: Новата улица
reading.text: Новата улица е хубава. Голямата къща е стара, но хубава. Новите коли са тук.
reading.translation: The new street is beautiful. The big house is old, but nice. The new cars are here.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe specific things using "the" on the adjective, for example: "Новата кола е хубава" or "Старото кафене е малко."

days.title: The definite article on masculine adjectives: новият

vocab.exampleEn: The new house is very big.

grammar.title: Masculine "the": новият (subject) vs новия (object)
grammar.explanation: On a **masculine** adjective the definite article has two written forms. Use the **full** form **-ият** when the phrase is the **subject** of the sentence (the doer): **Новият град е голям** (The new city is big). Use the **short** form **-ия** elsewhere, especially after a verb or preposition: **Виждам новия град** (I see the new city), **в новия дом** (in the new house). The difference is only in writing; both are pronounced nearly the same. With **голям** the article form is **големият / големия**.

reading.title: Новият град
reading.text: Новият град е голям и хубав. Виждам стария парк и новите къщи. Старият дом е тук, до парка.
reading.translation: The new city is big and beautiful. I see the old park and the new houses. The old house is here, next to the park.

days.speakingPrompt: Say one sentence where a masculine "the"-phrase is the subject (новият…) and one where it follows a verb (виждам новия…), for example: "Новият дом е голям. Виждам стария дом."

days.title: Review: describing things

grammar.title: Week 7 recap: agreement and the article on adjectives
grammar.explanation: Adjectives **agree** with their noun in gender and number. **нов** has four forms: **нов** (masc.), **нова** (fem.), **ново** (neuter), **нови** (plural) — and so do **голям, малък, хубав, стар**. The **definite article** (the *-the* ending) attaches to the **adjective**: feminine **-та** (**новата**), neuter **-то** (**новото**), plural **-те** (**новите**), and masculine **-ият/-ия** (**новият** as subject, **новия** after a verb or preposition). Don't forget the verb *to be*: **съм, си, е, сме, сте, са**.

examples.russian: нов дом, нова кола, ново кафене, нови къщи
examples.english: a new house, a new car, a new café, new houses

examples.russian: новата кола, новото кафене, новите къщи
examples.english: the new car, the new café, the new houses

examples.russian: Новият град е хубав. Виждам новия град.
examples.english: The new city is beautiful. I see the new city.

reading.title: Това е нашият град
reading.text: — Това е нов град, нали? — Да, новият град е голям и хубав. Виждам новите къщи и хубавите улици. А старият парк е тук, до колата.
reading.translation: — This is a new city, right? — Yes, the new city is big and beautiful. I see the new houses and the beautiful streets. And the old park is here, next to the car.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your neighbourhood for about a minute. Use at least one adjective in each form (нов/нова/ново/нови) and at least two adjectives with "the" attached (новата, новият, старите…), for example: "Новата улица е хубава. Старият дом е голям."
